crypto.name: X-Hub-Signature-256

key_source.key: The webhook secret token configured on the webhook.
key_source.note: Sent only if the webhook is configured with a secret. The signed content is the raw request body. Example value: sha256=757107ea0eb2509fc211221cce984b8a37570b6d7586c22c46f4379c8b043e17

payload_assembly.desc: Take the raw HTTP request body bytes exactly as received (do not re-serialize the JSON).
Compute HMAC-SHA256 over the raw body using the webhook secret as the HMAC key; hex-encode the digest and prefix it with 'sha256='.
Compare the result to the X-Hub-Signature-256 header using a constant-time comparison (never a plain == operator).

verify.desc: Recompute HMAC-SHA256 of the raw body with the stored secret, prefix 'sha256=', and compare in constant time. A legacy SHA-1 digest is also sent in X-Hub-Signature.

crypto.source: webhook-delivery-and-signing.md > Validating webhook deliveries; Delivery headers (X-Hub-Signature-256)

key_source.key: The webhook secret token configured on the webhook.
key_source.note: Legacy SHA-1 variant, sent only if a secret is configured. Prefer X-Hub-Signature-256.

verify.desc: HMAC hex digest of the request body generated using the SHA-1 hash function and the secret as the HMAC key.

callbacks.name: ping
callbacks.trigger: Sent when a new webhook is created, to confirm the configuration.
callbacks.transport: HTTP POST to the configured webhook URL; Content-Type: application/json; X-GitHub-Event: ping.
callbacks.verification: X-Hub-Signature-256 (HMAC-SHA256 of the raw body with the webhook secret), if a secret is configured.
callbacks.expected_response: HTTP 200 within 10 seconds. A non-200 or slower-than-10s response is recorded as a failed delivery. GitHub does NOT automatically redeliver; redeliver manually from the UI or via the REST API.
callbacks.source: webhook-events-and-payloads.md > Event: ping; webhook-delivery-and-signing.md > Handling failed deliveries

callbacks.name: star
callbacks.trigger: Sent when a repository is starred or unstarred (action = created | deleted).
callbacks.transport: HTTP POST to the configured webhook URL; Content-Type: application/json; X-GitHub-Event: star.
callbacks.verification: X-Hub-Signature-256 (HMAC-SHA256 of the raw body with the webhook secret), if a secret is configured.
callbacks.expected_response: HTTP 200 within 10 seconds. No automatic redelivery; manual or REST-API redelivery only.
callbacks.source: webhook-events-and-payloads.md > Event: star; webhook-delivery-and-signing.md > Handling failed deliveries

callbacks.name: push
callbacks.trigger: Sent when one or more commits are pushed to a repository branch or tag.
callbacks.transport: HTTP POST to the configured webhook URL; Content-Type: application/json; X-GitHub-Event: push.
callbacks.verification: X-Hub-Signature-256 (HMAC-SHA256 of the raw body with the webhook secret), if a secret is configured.
callbacks.expected_response: HTTP 200 within 10 seconds. No automatic redelivery; manual or REST-API redelivery only.
callbacks.source: webhook-events-and-payloads.md > Event: push; webhook-delivery-and-signing.md > Handling failed deliveries

field_conditions.rule: The X-Hub-Signature-256 and X-Hub-Signature headers are sent only when the webhook is configured with a secret.
field_conditions.source: webhook-delivery-and-signing.md > Delivery headers (X-Hub-Signature-256 'sent if the webhook is configured with a secret')

field_conditions.rule: starred_at is null when action = deleted (the star was removed).
field_conditions.source: webhook-events-and-payloads.md > Event: star (starred_at 'Will be null for the deleted action')

missing.detail: The webhook secret length/format and the exact byte encoding fed to HMAC (beyond 'the raw request body') are not specified in these sources.

There is no automatic-retry schedule because GitHub does not auto-redeliver; the receiver/integrator must drive redelivery via the REST API. The REST API redelivery endpoint path is not captured in these sources.
The sources do not ship full example payload bodies for ping/star/push, so no end-to-end request example is grounded here (object-typed properties such as repository/sender are not expanded).
